permutation_scheme: jointly permute HH and A_HEX across characters (preserves within-char HH-A_HEX dependence; breaks char-to-OCEAN linkage)
interpretation: p_one_sided is the fraction of null permutations producing as much or more stability as observed. If p < 0.05, the observed 93% stability exceeds what permuted-HEXACO would produce by chance.
_DEPRECATED: Superseded. This is the 5,000-permutation run (p = 0.536, reported as 0.54). The paper and notebook 02 use the 10,000-permutation run in cpm_rq10_ocean_hp_permutation_null.json (p = 0.526, reported as 0.53). Both are nulls; the 10k run is canonical because it is the one the notebook loads. Kept for provenance.
